claim_chain: TRD imposes major clinical and economic burdens
Previous evaluations compared esketamine only with unrealistic comparators
Our model incorporates a broader spectrum of comparators reflecting actual clinical practice
Esketamine was not cost-effective compared to most treatment strategies under the WTP threshold
Combination therapy emerged as the most cost-effective option
Cost-effectiveness evidence may help inform more context-sensitive treatment sequencing strategies
Policy approaches such as price negotiation and optimized service delivery may improve the value of esketamine

typical_pattern: State main finding or comparison result
Contextualize with prior literature or methodological differences
Explain discrepancies or mechanisms driving the result
Support interpretation with sensitivity analysis or additional evidence
State implications for practice or policy

likely_priorities: Validity of indirect comparisons and heterogeneity of trial populations
Generalizability of findings to different healthcare systems and labor cost structures
Robustness of cost-effectiveness conclusions across sensitivity analyses
Appropriateness of effectiveness assumptions for ECT and other comparators
Clinical relevance of model structure and treatment pathways
likely_attacks: Reliance on indirect comparisons and assumptions derived from heterogeneous clinical trial populations
Conservative estimation of ECT effectiveness not restricted to severe clinical subgroups
Generalizability limited to high-income healthcare systems with high labor costs
Potential underestimation of QALY gains for labor-intensive interventions like ECT
Lack of real-world head-to-head comparative effectiveness data
Uncertainty in long-term relapse and remission transition probabilities
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states that the main limitation is the reliance on indirect comparisons and assumptions derived from heterogeneous clinical trial populations. The discussion extensively addresses methodological differences with prior studies, particularly regarding ECT cost structure assumptions and effectiveness modeling choices. The authors acknowledge that ECT efficacy parameters were not consistently restricted to the most severe clinical subgroups, potentially leading to conservative QALY estimates. The paper emphasizes that findings should be interpreted in the context of a high-income healthcare system with relatively high labor costs, and that generalizability to lower-labor-cost systems may differ substantially. The authors also note that the component-level cost reporting enables context-specific re-estimation and supports adaptation to alternative healthcare settings.

examples.quoted_phrase: esketamine was not cost-effective compared to most other treatment strategies under the WTP threshold
combination therapy emerged as the most cost-effective option
may not fully reflect real-world patient characteristics and treatment pathways

phrase_evidence.phrase: In this study, we compared esketamine nasal spray with six commonly used third-line treatment strategies for TRD
our model incorporates a broader spectrum of both pharmacological and nonpharmacological comparators, better reflecting actual clinical practice
The base-case results indicated that esketamine was not cost-effective compared to most other treatment strategies under the WTP threshold
combination therapy emerged as the most cost-effective option
Our results are broadly consistent with prior economic evaluations
This discrepancy is not unexpected and can be largely explained by two key methodological differences
substantial differences exist in how ECT costs are defined across studies
This interpretation is further supported by our DSA
these findings underscore that differences in healthcare financing structures and labor cost accounting can materially affect cost-effectiveness estimates
highlight the importance of context-specific costing in economic evaluations
may further contribute to divergent findings
the incremental QALY gains associated with ECT may be conservatively estimated
Taken together, these considerations also have important implications for the generalizability of our findings
Our findings should be interpreted in the context of a high-income healthcare system with relatively high labor costs
the relative cost-effectiveness of these interventions may differ substantially
sensitivity and scenario analyses did not materially alter the conclusions of the base-case results
DSA identified the efficacy parameters of esketamine as the most influential drivers of cost-effectiveness
even under the most favorable assumptions, the resulting ICER still remained above the WTP threshold
PSA further confirmed the limited cost-effectiveness of esketamine
The main limitation of this study is the reliance on indirect comparisons and assumptions derived from heterogeneous clinical trial populations
which may not fully reflect real-world patient characteristics and treatment pathways
These findings suggest that cost-effectiveness evidence may help inform more context-sensitive treatment sequencing strategies
Policy approaches such as price negotiation, optimized service delivery, and alternative dosing strategies may improve the value of esketamine
Each point represents a third-line treatment strategy for TRD compared with augmentation therapy
Ellipses indicate 95% uncertainty intervals
Curves show the probability that each treatment strategy is the most cost-effective across a range of willingness-to-pay thresholds
This design enhances both the contextual relevance and the generalizability of our findings to routine clinical settings
necessitating an estimated 60% price reduction to achieve cost-effectiveness
primarily due to productivity gains from rapid functional improvement and reduced work loss
making their economic value sensitive to local implementation practices

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.

_phrase_violations.phrase: DSA identified the efficacy parameters of esketamine as the most influential drivers of cost-effectiveness
Curves show the probability that each treatment strategy is the most cost-effective across a range of willingness-to-pay thresholds
